description: Phase-8 inbound-email triage record (ADR-0015). EXACTLY one row per email arriving at the 3 shared inboxes -- the universal 'we saw this' record. The new lightweight triage table chosen over extending Case (which would pollute every case tally/dedup/readiness with non-work rows and still could not represent a row that will never become a case) or tags-only (no queryable triage queue). Every email is categorised; there is NO drop-junk pre-filter (spam/auto-replies/newsletters fall through to other). A receiving_work row points at the Case the existing intake chain creates; query/other rows hold metadata + a mailbox pointer only (NO .eml persisted to Blob for query/other -- the mailbox keeps the mail). Dataverse holds metadata only; Dataverse stays the system of record (ADR-0012).

lifecycle.note: Phase-8 Phase-A. Authored offline, applied live by the operator via 26-inbound-email.ps1. The triage-classify flow + /classify-email route are wired separately; the BOX_*/EMAIL_AI gates governing later phases stay OFF.

primaryColumn.description: Human-friendly label for the triage row (e.g. subject snippet + from). Not the dedup key; cr1bd_sourcemessageid is the alternate key.

columns.description: DEDUP KEY (the triage dedup anchor; mirrors cr1bd_case.cr1bd_sourcemessageid). Graph / Internet Message-ID of the originating email. The generalised intake dedup probes BOTH cr1bd_cases and cr1bd_inboundemail so a repeat query/other email is dropped too (ADR-0010). Backs the get-or-create idempotency guard (alternate key).
The email subject line (plain text). Part of the classifier haystack.
The sender's email address (informational; 320 = RFC max). Passed to /classify-email as from_address.

The sender's domain (after @). The provider-match key reused from the intake flow's Init_senderDomain / Filter_exact_domain (domain match only, NO alias matching). ONE -> existing provider, NONE -> new client, AMBIGUOUS -> existing-but-unassigned.
The shared inbox the email arrived on (the soft-rollout enables one inbox first). Mirrors cr1bd_case.cr1bd_sourcemailbox.
When the email was received (the triage queue's age / ordering key).
Whether the email carried any attachments (the V3 trigger body/hasAttachments). An instruction attachment is the single strongest work signal; the body-only path fires only when this is false.
The triage CATEGORY returned by /classify-email: receiving_work | query | other. Members mirror email_classifier.py CATEGORY_* string values 1:1; append-only (never renumber).

The triage SUBTYPE returned by /classify-email: existing_provider_instruction | existing_provider_audit | new_client_work | query_existing_work | query_new_enquiry | other. Members mirror email_classifier.py SUBTYPE_* string values 1:1; append-only. (existing_provider_audit is the inbound SUBTYPE for an audit case -- distinct from the cr1bd_auditevent action LOG and the ADR-0014 case-TYPE 'audit'.)

The classifier's coarse confidence band 0.0-1.0 (email_classifier.py _CONFIDENCE_* -- 0.95 strong / 0.8 good / 0.6 weak / 0.3 abstain). Lets the later gated LLM pass (Phase C) target only low-confidence / other rows without re-deriving a score. Float (Double) preserves the exact band value.

Which engine produced the label: deterministic | llm | human. Phase-A is always deterministic; the gated Phase-C triage-llm child writes llm; a staff reclassify writes human. Kept as a short String token (not a choiceset) -- a 3-value low-churn provenance flag.

JSON / newline list of the EXACT rule ids and fired phrases the classifier returned (email_classifier.py signals[], e.g. work_keywords:..., audit_phrases:..., rule:instruction_doc_audit). Same explainability discipline as detect_audit_signals -- every triage decision is auditable.

The triage lifecycle: new | routed | actioned | dismissed. new on creation; routed once the Switch routes a receiving_work row to the Case chain (or a query is linked to its open Case); actioned/dismissed set by a human in the Phase-B Inbox/Triage screen. Kept as a short String token (not a choiceset) -- a small low-churn workflow flag, distinct from cr1bd_casestatus.

The first VRM the classifier found in subject+body (email_classifier.py body_vrm, via the engine VRM_RE). The flow runs the open-Case Dataverse lookup itself -- Case/PO FIRST, VRM only as a fallback (ADR-0002). Closes the 'instructions typed in the email body, no attachment' gap. The classifier NEVER auto-links; it only reports what it found.

The first Case/PO the classifier found in subject+body (email_classifier.py body_caseref, via CASEREF_RE: principalCode + 2-digit year + 3-digit sequence, optional A. audit prefix). The PRIMARY open-Case link key the flow resolves before falling back to the VRM (each accident has its own Case/PO -> its own Box folder). True ambiguity is rare; never silently merge (ADR-0010).
A short html-stripped plain-text preview of the body for the Phase-B triage UI (query/other rows show this + an open-in-mailbox pointer, since no .eml is persisted to Blob for query/other -- A7).

-> Case (NULLABLE). Set for a receiving_work row (the Case the existing intake chain creates -- written back after routing) OR for the OPEN Case a query_existing_work row is about (linked by Case/PO first, VRM fallback; the flow confirms the link and NEVER silently merges -- ADR-0010). Null for new_client_work / query_new_enquiry / other.
-> WorkProvider (NULLABLE). The provider matched by sender domain (provider_match_state=one). Null when the domain matched none (new client) or was ambiguous.

alternateKeys.description: DEDUP ANCHOR. Idempotency guard for the triage get-or-create (prevents a double triage row on Graph retries / re-sends). Exact Message-ID repeat -> drop (ADR-0010). Mirrors cr1bd_case_sourcemessageid_key.

notes: Taxonomy is two stable, append-only choicesets (cr1bd_inboundcategory + cr1bd_inboundsubtype) whose members mirror functions/parser/cedocumentmapper_v2/rules/email_classifier.py CATEGORY_* / SUBTYPE_* 1:1. NEVER renumber (same discipline as case-status.json / audit-event.json).

The classifier (/classify-email) is PURE -- it surfaces cr1bd_bodycaseref + cr1bd_bodyvrm but NEVER auto-links a Case. The open-Case lookup (Case/PO first, VRM fallback) stays on the flow side, mirroring how /parse leaves 'which Case' to the flow.

Lookups cr1bd_caseid + cr1bd_workproviderid are both NULLABLE; the 1:N relationships (cr1bd_case_inboundemail, cr1bd_workprovider_inboundemail) are declared in relationships.json with delete=RemoveLink so a removed Case/Provider does not delete the audit-of-record triage row.

Storage is negligible (~2 KB/row, ~36k rows/yr ~ 0.07 GB -> $0 within entitlement). The operational watch is Power Automate run volume once the trigger drops fetchOnlyWithAttachment (a queue monitor at concurrency=1, NOT a cost ceiling) -- not a reason for a drop-junk pre-filter.

A raw .eml is persisted to Azure Blob ONLY for the receiving_work (Case-extracted) path via classify-persist. For query/other, no .eml is persisted -- the mailbox keeps the mail and this row holds metadata + a pointer (cr1bd_sourcemessageid / cr1bd_sourcemailbox). Box stays OFF the triage critical path (all BOX_* gates false; a future gated BOX_QUERY_ARCHIVE_ENABLED is deferred, not built).
